ideas.title: Field Day Debrief: What Actually Worked and What Didn't
ideas.why_now: Field Day 2026 just wrapped and operators are actively sharing lessons, scores, and stories across the community.
ideas.angle: Skip the trophy talk. Focus on the practical takeaways everyday operators can steal for next year: gear choices, site setup, operating rhythm, and the stuff nobody warns you about before your first Field Day.

sources.label: Mastodon #hamradio, Field Day lessons thread
Mastodon #fieldday, CW captain after-action
Mastodon #fieldday, 100+ attendees stat post

ideas.title: Commercial Operators on Amateur Bands: Should You Be Worried?
ideas.why_now: IARU press release this week sparked alarm about commercial entities gaining access to amateur spectrum.
ideas.angle: Break down what the IARU announcement actually says versus the fear, and help everyday hams understand what spectrum sharing could mean for their favorite frequencies in practical terms.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, spectrum worry post
Mastodon #amateurradio, IARU earthquake frequency clearance context

ideas.title: How to Run a QRPp POTA Activation on Basically Nothing
ideas.why_now: Multiple operators this week posted QRPp CW POTA activations using under 200mW, generating real curiosity in replies.
ideas.angle: Walk through what sub-1-watt POTA actually looks like: which bands hold up, how CW changes the math, and whether a 9V battery activation is a stunt or a legitimate operating strategy.

sources.label: Mastodon #morsecode, 9V battery QCX Mini activation
Mastodon #morsecode, QRPp POTA with 200mW
Mastodon #morsecode, early bird activation post

ideas.title: Free CW Training Resources That Are Actually Good
ideas.why_now: Multiple threads this week asked for Morse code training recommendations, with LICW also promoting Field Day CW prep sessions.
ideas.angle: Round up the best free tools including LCWO, the Morserino, and community practice sessions. Give beginners a real on-ramp and tell intermediate ops what actually moves the needle past the plateau.

sources.label: Mastodon #morsecode, request for free CW training resources
Mastodon #morsecode, learning basics on breaks at work
Mastodon #fieldday, LICW June CW training sessions

ideas.title: Ham Radio on the Road: Operating From Dispersed Campsites
ideas.why_now: A practical guide to dispersed camping radio setups surfaced this week alongside active summer portable operating posts.
ideas.angle: Cover the real logistics: how to anchor antennas without park infrastructure, power management away from trailheads, and whether a dispersed site counts for POTA. Practical and adventurous without requiring expensive gear.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, secure radio setup for dispersed camping
Mastodon #hamradio, portable op with coyote interruption
Mastodon #amateurradio, POTA parks activation story

ideas.title: Wavelog 3.0 Is Out: Is It Time to Ditch Your Old Logger?
ideas.why_now: Wavelog 3.0.0 released this week, making it a timely moment to evaluate self-hosted logging options for portable and home stations.
ideas.angle: Compare what Wavelog 3.0 now offers against popular alternatives like Log4OM and HAMRS. Focus on what matters to the everyday operator: easy POTA upload, mobile-friendly logging, and not needing a server admin degree.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, Wavelog 3.0.0 release announcement

ideas.title: Venezuela Earthquake: When Ham Radio Is the Only Link
ideas.why_now: IARU issued a frequency clearance request this week following a fatal Venezuela earthquake, with hams in the Americas called to help.
ideas.angle: Use this real-world activation as a practical primer on emergency frequency clearance, what EMCOMM readiness actually looks like for a regular ham, and how to be useful rather than just on the air when disaster hits.

sources.label: Mastodon #amateurradio, IARU frequency clearance request
Mastodon #amateurradio, Venezuela earthquake ham radio response
Mastodon #qrp, EMCOM thread on off-grid radio readiness

ideas.title: Powering Your Portable Rig From a USB Power Bank
ideas.why_now: A direct question about USB PD to 12V solutions for QRP portable ops generated discussion this week and reflects a very common beginner pain point.
ideas.angle: Test and explain the real options: buck-boost converters, PD trigger boards, and purpose-built solutions. Give listeners a clear recommendation for powering a QRP rig from gear they likely already own.

sources.label: Mastodon #qrp, USB PD to 12V question for portable ops
Mastodon #morsecode, Penntek TR-45L portable summer plans
